name: Intercom API
what_it_does: Intercom's REST API for managing customer conversations, contacts, companies, and automated messaging workflows in Intercom's customer service platform.
use_cases: Creating and replying to customer conversations from agent workflows
Managing contact and company data for CRM operations
Creating and routing support tickets via automation
Triggering automated message sequences from agent events
Building AI-powered customer support bots integrated with Intercom
not_for: Simple email-only support (Intercom is a full platform)
Teams on tight budgets (Intercom is expensive)
best_when: An agent needs to read or respond to customer conversations in Intercom, or manage contact/company data for customer success workflows.
avoid_when: You don't use Intercom, or need a simple helpdesk without Intercom's in-app messaging capabilities.

auth.notes: Access tokens for single-workspace integrations. OAuth2 for multi-workspace apps with scope-based permissions. Token-based auth is simplest for agents. Tokens scope to one workspace.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: very limited, early-stage startups only
pricing.notes: One of the more expensive customer support platforms. Startup program offers free access. Most businesses will need Essential ($39/seat) minimum for API access.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include type, message, and errors array. API versioning is explicit via header. Error codes are well-documented in reference. Generally agent-friendly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Conversation creation and replies are not idempotent on retry.
known_agent_gotchas: API versioning is required via Intercom-Version header — must specify correct version
Reply to conversation requires correct author type (bot vs admin) — bots need specific setup
Conversations vs tickets have different data models — check which is needed
Contact deduplication by email is not automatic — agents may create duplicate contacts
Webhook signatures must be validated for security
Rate limits are per workspace, not per token — shared limits with other integrations
